description:
A JavaScript web project is located at /home/user/my-web-app.  
  Your goal is to perform a **minor semantic-version bump** and register that change in the project changelog.

  Starting state of the repository (already present on disk):
  • /home/user/my-web-app/package.json  
    {
      "name": "my-web-app",
      "version": "1.2.3",
      "description": "Sample web app",
      "dependencies": {}
    }

  • /home/user/my-web-app/CHANGELOG.md  
    # Changelog

    All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

    ## [1.2.3] - 2023-07-01
    - Initial release.

  Required final state:

  1. The version field in /home/user/my-web-app/package.json must be bumped **exactly** to 1.3.0 (a minor bump).  
     No other keys or formatting within package.json may be changed.

  2. The file /home/user/my-web-app/CHANGELOG.md must have a **new release section inserted at the top of the file** with the following exact Markdown block (including blank lines and indentation):

    ## [1.3.0] - 2024-01-01
    ### Added
    - Implemented search feature on the homepage.

    (there must be a single blank line after that block before the former “## [1.2.3] …” section begins).

    When the task is complete, the entire contents of /home/user/my-web-app/CHANGELOG.md should read **exactly**:

    # Changelog

    All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

    ## [1.3.0] - 2024-01-01
    ### Added
    - Implemented search feature on the homepage.

    ## [1.2.3] - 2023-07-01
    - Initial release.

  3. Create a plain-text verification file at /home/user/version_bump.log with this one-line content (do not add extra spaces or line breaks):
    1.3.0

  The whole job should require no more than 3–4 terminal commands.  
  Make sure you run those commands from inside /home/user/my-web-app so relative paths work as expected.
